name: Pour Concrete
description: Pouring a concrete slab, sidewalk, patio, or driveway
tips: Pour in early morning to allow a full day of curing
Keep concrete above 50°F for 48 hours after pouring
Use curing blankets if overnight temps drop near freezing

name: Pour Footings/Foundation
description: Pouring structural footings or a foundation
tips: Footings need 48+ hours above freezing to gain structural strength
Protect with insulating blankets if overnight temps drop below 50°F
Avoid pouring into saturated soil — drain or delay

name: Lay Brick/Block
description: Laying brick or concrete block with mortar
tips: Mortar needs temps above 40°F to cure properly
Cover fresh work overnight if frost is possible
Keep brick moist in hot, dry weather to prevent rapid set

name: Repoint Mortar
description: Tuck-pointing or repairing mortar joints
tips: Keep new joints damp for several days to slow cure
Avoid direct sun on hot days — work in shade
Pre-wet old joints so they don't pull moisture from fresh mortar

name: Apply Stucco
description: Applying stucco coats to exterior walls
tips: Keep stucco damp for the first 48 hours to prevent cracking
Avoid applying in full sun on hot, dry days
Plan for 48+ hours of dry weather after each coat

name: Seal Concrete/Driveway
description: Applying a sealer to a concrete driveway, patio, or walk
tips: Surface must be fully dry — no damp patches
Apply in morning so sealer cures before overnight dew
Keep foot traffic off for at least 24 hours

name: Exterior Paint (Latex)
description: Painting exterior surfaces with latex (water-based) paint
tips: Latex needs the air temp at least 5°F above the dew point to form a good film
Stop 2 hours before dew sets in — evening painting invites failures
Don't paint in direct sun on hot days — paint skins too fast

name: Exterior Paint (Oil-Based)
description: Painting exterior surfaces with oil-based paint
tips: Oil-based paint tolerates cooler temperatures than latex
Plan for 24 hours of dry weather after the final coat
Ventilate well and wear a respirator for solvent fumes

name: Stain Deck (Oil-Based)
description: Applying an oil-based deck stain or sealer
tips: Apply in morning so it has all day to soak in and dry
Wood must be clean and dry — washed boards need 48 hours
Skip days with rising humidity past noon

name: Stain Deck (Water-Based)
description: Applying a water-based deck stain or sealer
tips: Water-based stains are humidity sensitive — aim for 40–70%
Back-brush to work stain into the grain
Recoat same day only if first coat is fully absorbed

name: Stain/Seal Fence
description: Staining or sealing a wood fence
tips: Work on the shady side as the sun moves across the fence
Pressure-washed fences need 48 hours to dry before staining
Two thin coats beat one heavy coat

name: Paint Outdoor Furniture
description: Painting chairs, tables, or other outdoor furniture
tips: Sand and prime bare spots for lasting adhesion
Paint upside-down first to catch the undersides and joints
Light coats beat heavy coats — expect 2–3 passes

name: Apply Exterior Caulk
description: Applying exterior caulk around windows, trim, or siding
tips: Surface must be dry and clean of old, loose caulk
Tool the bead within a few minutes of applying
Check manufacturer label for temperature and cure time specifics

name: Paint/Epoxy Garage Floor
description: Applying a floor paint or epoxy coating to a garage slab
tips: Concrete must be fully cured (30+ days) and moisture-tested
Etch or grind for proper adhesion
Keep garage ventilated — epoxy fumes linger

name: Install Asphalt Shingles
description: Installing asphalt shingles on a sloped roof
tips: Shingles need warmth to seal — below 40°F use hand tabs or wait
Hot days soften shingles — watch for scuff marks from boots
High winds make staging dangerous — stop work at gusts above 25 mph

name: Apply Roof Sealant
description: Applying a roof sealant or coating
tips: Apply to clean, dry roofing — morning dew can ruin adhesion
Check the label for recoat window and overnight temperature minimums
Avoid applying right before overnight condensation forms

name: Install Metal Roofing
description: Installing metal roof panels
tips: Metal panels act like sails — stop work in gusty wind
Mark cuts carefully; field shears leave sharp edges
Wear grippy footwear — metal gets slick with any moisture

name: Patch/Tar Roof
description: Patching a roof leak with tar or flashing cement
tips: Sweep debris and dry the area before applying
Layer fabric reinforcement into the wet patch for longevity
A temporary tarp is a better emergency fix than rushing a tar patch

name: Seed Lawn (Cool-Season)
description: Seeding fescue, bluegrass, or ryegrass lawns
tips: Light rain after seeding helps germination — avoid heavy downpours
Keep seed consistently moist for 14–21 days
Early fall is the ideal window in most zones

name: Seed Lawn (Warm-Season)
description: Seeding bermuda, zoysia, or other warm-season grasses
tips: Late spring to early summer is the sweet spot
Consistent moisture matters more than frequency — use light, frequent watering
Soil temps above 65°F are the real indicator

name: Lay Sod
description: Installing sod on a prepared bed
tips: Water sod within 30 minutes of laying — it dries out fast
Stagger seams like bricks and roll the new lawn to press out air
Keep foot traffic off for two weeks

name: Apply Fertilizer
description: Applying granular or liquid fertilizer to a lawn
tips: A light rain within 24 hours helps wash granules into soil
Skip if heavy rain is forecast — fertilizer runoff pollutes waterways
Don't apply to drought-stressed, dormant lawns

name: Apply Herbicide
description: Spraying herbicide on weeds
tips: Drift damages neighboring plants — hold off if gusts exceed 10 mph
Most products need 4–6 dry hours to absorb
Morning applications avoid midday heat stress on weeds

name: Apply Pesticide
description: Spraying pesticide on plants or a lawn
tips: Spray in the cooler parts of the day to protect pollinators
Read the label — rainfast times vary widely between products
Wear the PPE the label calls for, every time

name: Plant Trees/Shrubs
description: Planting balled-and-burlapped or container trees and shrubs
tips: Dig the hole twice as wide as the root ball, same depth
Water deeply the first season — the roots haven't found their own supply
Mulch 2–3 inches around the base, pulled back from the trunk

name: Mulch Garden Beds
description: Spreading mulch around beds and borders
tips: 2–3 inches is enough — deeper mulch smothers roots
Pull mulch back a few inches from stems and trunks
Windy days turn fine mulch into a mess

name: Aerate Lawn
description: Core aerating a lawn to relieve compaction
tips: Soil should be moist — not saturated, not bone dry
Water the day before for best plug pull depth
Aerate in the active growing season so the lawn heals quickly

name: Install Wood Fence
description: Building a wood fence with set posts
tips: Post concrete needs above-freezing temps for a full 48 hours
Set posts plumb and brace them until the concrete cures
Leave expansion gaps between pickets for wet weather

name: Build Deck Structure
description: Framing a deck with joists and beams
tips: Pressure-treated lumber is heavy when wet — stage it under cover
Use hot-dip galvanized or stainless fasteners for modern PT lumber
Check joist hangers with the specified nails, not deck screws

name: Install Composite Decking
description: Installing composite or PVC deck boards
tips: Composites expand and contract with temperature — leave the manufacturer's gap
Pre-drill ends to prevent mushrooming
Boards are dark and hot in the sun — mind your tool parts

name: Outdoor Wood Gluing
description: Gluing wood joints outdoors with exterior-rated adhesive
tips: Use Type II or Type III exterior wood glue
Clamp for at least 30 minutes, leave overnight before stress
Wipe squeeze-out immediately — dry glue resists stain

name: Seal Asphalt Driveway
description: Applying a sealcoat to an asphalt driveway
tips: Driveway surface must be 50°F+ for 24 hours before and after
Fill cracks and clean oil stains before sealing
Plan for 48 hours of dry, above-freezing weather

name: Asphalt Paving
description: Laying hot-mix asphalt
tips: Hot-mix temps drop fast — coordinate loads and crew
Compact before the mix falls below 185°F
Don't pave over wet or frozen base material

name: Lay Pavers
description: Installing concrete or stone pavers
tips: Screed a flat, compacted sand bed — small errors multiply
Cut edges with a wet saw and dust mask
Sweep polymeric sand only on a dry forecast day

name: Wash & Wax Car
description: Washing and waxing a car outdoors
tips: Wash in shade — direct sun bakes soap into streaks
Dry panel by panel to avoid water spots
Apply wax in thin coats on a cool surface

name: Spray Paint (Outdoor)
description: Outdoor spray painting — cans or HVLP
tips: Overspray drifts farther than you'd guess — mask neighboring surfaces
Warm the can in your hand (not hot water) for smoother flow
Hold 8–12 inches off the surface and keep the can moving

name: Transplant Seedlings
description: Moving seedlings from trays into the garden
tips: Harden off for 7–10 days before transplanting
Move seedlings on overcast afternoons to reduce shock
Water in immediately with a gentle stream

name: Spray Fungicide
description: Applying a fungicide to plants
tips: Apply before symptoms spread — fungicides are mostly preventative
Coat both sides of the leaves
Rotate active ingredients to avoid resistance

name: Prune Trees/Shrubs
description: Pruning trees or shrubs
tips: Wet conditions spread disease through open cuts — skip rainy days
Sanitize pruners between plants, especially with any fungal concern
Make cuts just outside the branch collar

name: Start Garden Bed / Till
description: Tilling a new bed or turning a garden over
tips: Squeeze a handful of soil — if it holds a ball and oozes water, wait
Till in compost while turning for an instant amendment
Work in multiple shallow passes rather than one deep one

name: Power Wash House/Deck
description: Power washing siding, decks, or walkways
tips: Surfaces need 24+ hours to dry before painting or staining
Use the widest tip that does the job — narrow tips cut wood
Never spray up under siding — water gets trapped behind

name: Clean Gutters
description: Cleaning out gutters and downspouts
tips: A sturdy ladder with standoff arms is safer than leaning on the gutter
Flush downspouts with a hose after clearing
Never climb in gusty wind — reschedule

name: Install Outdoor Lighting
description: Installing exterior or landscape lighting
tips: Kill power at the breaker — a tester is a few dollars
Use weatherproof connections at every splice
Test circuits before burying low-voltage wire

name: Resurface Pool
description: Plastering, painting, or resurfacing a pool interior
tips: Plaster pools need to be refilled within hours of finishing
Plan for 72+ hours of mild, dry weather
Cover the pool overnight if dew or cool temps are expected

name: Set Fence Posts (Concrete)
description: Setting fence posts in concrete footings
tips: Brace posts plumb and leave bracing for at least 24 hours
Dome the top of the concrete to shed water away from the post
Mix bagged concrete slightly stiff — too wet weakens the set
